questions.scenario: The team uses Cloud Run for the Orders API. Which responsibility still belongs to the team?

options.text: Choosing runtime configuration, service account, traffic behavior, and health expectations.
Manually patching every host that runs the serverless platform.
Creating a new GCP organization for every request.

questions.explanation: Cloud Run removes server management from the team, but the team still owns how the service is configured and operated.

questions.scenario: The team uses Cloud SQL. What is the safest way to describe responsibility?

options.text: GCP manages much of the database platform, while the team still owns data model, access, backup choices, and restore practice.
GCP owns the business meaning of every order row.
The team no longer needs to test database recovery.
Cloud SQL removes the need for application permissions.

questions.explanation: Managed database services handle platform work, but the team still owns data meaning, access, and recovery promises.

questions.scenario: Which choices are still team-owned design decisions in a managed GCP system?

options.text: Which project and billing account own production.
Which service account the app uses at runtime.
Which logs and metrics prove checkout is healthy.
Whether Google Cloud should have physical servers.

questions.explanation: Project ownership, identity, and evidence are team decisions. The provider owns the underlying infrastructure machinery.
